text:
Artificial intelligence is changing how we think about creativity. Today, AI can generate paintings, compose music, and even write stories or poems. For example, someone can type a few words into a program, and in seconds, it creates a unique piece of art or a short story. This has led to interesting questions about human creativity versus machine creativity.

Some people argue that AI simply copies patterns from existing works, so it isn’t truly creative. Others believe it offers new ways to inspire human artists. While AI can produce impressive results quickly, it lacks human emotions and personal experiences. Real creativity may still require the feelings, mistakes, and imagination that only people can bring.
